key_insight: A unified, energy-based CutFEM formulation for finite-strain elasticity uses automatic differentiation to derive stresses and tangents from any hyperelastic energy, while keeping stability and convergence cut-independent.
engineering_relevance: This enables robust unfitted solid mechanics simulations on complex geometries without re-deriving constitutive laws, with provable conditioning and accuracy comparable to fitted methods.
method: Build a fully variational augmented energy: bulk hyperelastic energy + Nitsche boundary terms + ghost-penalty stabilization.
Use automatic differentiation to obtain the first Piola–Kirchhoff stress and consistent tangent directly from the scalar energy density.
Analyze the linearized Newton problem for cut-independent coercivity/continuity and quasi-optimal convergence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth test case, plus recovered optimal rates after local mesh refinement near corner singularities.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be capped by true corner singularities at mixed Dirichlet–Neumann junctions unless the mesh is locally refined, and the method relies on careful stabilization parameter choices.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
